535	def _plan_expert_facet_queries(
536	    question: str,
537	    llm: LlmFn,
538	    per_query_retrieve: PerQueryRetrieveFn,
539	    *,
540	    max_queries: int | None = None,
541	    retrieve_kwargs: dict | None = None,
542	    retrieval_deadline_monotonic: "float | None" = None,
543	) -> tuple[list[str], list[Any]]:
544	    """R1+R2 (I-deepfix-001, #1344) facet-driven frontier: seed queries from the expert-facet tree,
545	    issue them directly, then (when R2 is enabled) run the facet-completeness expansion loop over the
546	    UNCOVERED facets until the source yield saturates. Returns (queries_issued, per_query_results),
547	    the SAME contract as ``plan_fs_researcher_queries``.
548	
549	    R1 widens the frontier (facet x angle, scope-anchored) — the largest single DRB-II recall lever.
550	    R2 keys the completeness/expansion loop to the TASK's own facets so a general (non-clinical) task
551	    no longer reads a vacuous "0 of 0" and fires real gap-closing retrieval. Both ADD on-topic queries
552	    only and DROP ZERO sources; the FS-Researcher ``max_queries`` cap (compute-safety) still bounds
553	    cost; the R2 saturation stop is yield-keyed, never a breadth count (§-1.3).
554	    """
555	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import expert_facet_planner as _efp
556	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import facet_completeness as _fc
557	
558	    max_queries = max_queries or _max_queries()
559	    retrieve_kwargs = dict(retrieve_kwargs or {})
560	
561	    queries: list[str] = []
562	    results: list[Any] = []
563	    seen_q: set[str] = set()
564	    corpus_rows: list[dict] = []
565	
566	    _deadline = retrieval_deadline_monotonic
567	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 (#1344): seconds of wall-time consumed by the ADDITIVE landmark-study
568	    # planning lane, credited BACK to the wall so that lane rides OUTSIDE the baseline retrieval budget
569	    # and never displaces a baseline query (Codex/Fable P1 "or add budget"). A mutable box so the nested
570	    # ``_wall_passed`` reads the running total; it stays 0.0 (byte-identical) unless the landmark lane fires.
571	    _extra_lane_credit = [0.0]
572	
573	    def _wall_passed() -> bool:
574	        _d = _deadline
575	        if _d is not None and _extra_lane_credit[0]:
576	            _d = _d + _extra_lane_credit[0]
577	        return _retrieval_deadline_passed(_d)
578	
579	    if _wall_passed():
580	        return queries, results
581	
582	    # R1: build the facet tree (one bounded LLM call) and its scope-anchored angle queries.
583	    facets = _efp.plan_expert_facets(question, llm)
584	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3a (#1344): expert-facet-planner ACTIVATION fire marker. Emitted ONLY when
585	    # PG_EXPERT_FACET_PLANNER is ON (this whole facet path is reached only under the flag; the guard keeps
586	    # the marker OFF byte-identical even if a test drives this helper directly). facets=0 with the flag ON
587	    # is the eligible-yet-zero (degenerate-LLM-reply) signal. Structural presence + count (§-1.3).
588	    if _efp.expert_facet_enabled():
589	        logger.info("[activation] expert_facet_planner: facets=%d", len(facets))
590	
591	    # R1+R2 seed/reserve split (I-deepfix-001, #1344). Seed BREADTH-FIRST (every facet's
592	    # primary angle before any facet's deeper angle) so the query budget spreads across ALL
593	    # facets — fixing the measured "only 7 of 35 facets seeded" starvation. When the R2
594	    # completeness loop is ON, seed only the first `_seed_angles_per_facet()` angle(s) per
595	    # facet and RESERVE each facet's remaining angles for the R2 expansion loop to fire on
596	    # the facets the seed corpus leaves UNCOVERED. The prior code registered EVERY angle as a
597	    # seed, so `run_facet_expansion` (drawing from the SAME `facet.queries`) always read
598	    # "frontier exhausted" and issued ZERO expansion queries — the Codex/Fable P1. When R2 is
599	    # OFF the full angle frontier is seeded (the R1-only path is unchanged in count).
600	    _r2_on = _fc.facet_completeness_enabled()
601	    _seed_angle_limit = _seed_angles_per_facet() if _r2_on else None
602	    seed_queries = _breadth_first_seeds(facets, _seed_angle_limit)
603	
604	    # R5 (I-deepfix-001, #1344): multilingual / cross-lingual frontier. On a
605	    # non-English (e.g. zh) DRB-II task the English facet queries never reach the
606	    # native-language primaries; detect the task's language profile and ADD
607	    # on-language queries (English stays first + unchanged) so a native-language
608	    # source and its English paraphrase land in the SAME multi-backend retrieval,
609	    # cross-lingually reranked into one consolidation basket. English-only tasks
610	    # are byte-identical (the expansion returns the seeds unchanged); default-ON,
611	    # OFF via PG_MULTILINGUAL_RETRIEVAL. §-1.3: adds on-language queries only,
612	    # drops ZERO sources, touches no faithfulness gate.
613	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import language_profile as _lp
614	    if _lp.multilingual_enabled():
615	        _profile = _lp.detect_language_profile(question)
616	        if _profile.is_multilingual:
617	            # Inject a production translator wrapping the GLM policy so an EXPLICIT
618	            # -language task (all-ASCII English body + "Answer in Chinese") — which has
619	            # no native script to carry — still emits real native-language queries.
620	            _translate_fn = _make_multilingual_translate_fn(
621	                llm, list(seed_queries), _profile.non_english
622	            )
623	            _expanded = _lp.expand_queries_for_profile(
624	                seed_queries, _profile, question, translate_fn=_translate_fn
625	            )
626	            # Separate the native additions from the English seeds, then RESERVE a
627	            # bounded slice of native queries inside the query budget so a wide English
628	            # R1 frontier cannot starve the task's own language out of the issued set.
629	            _eng_keys = {(q or "").strip().lower() for q in seed_queries}
630	            _native_adds = [
631	                q for q in _expanded if (q or "").strip().lower() not in _eng_keys
632	            ]
633	            seed_queries = _reserve_native_within_budget(
634	                list(seed_queries), _native_adds, max_queries
635	            )
636	
637	    # R2 entity-qgen (I-deepfix-001, #1344): STORM-style sub-entity + perspective query expansion.
638	    # The abstract facet frontier names sub-TOPICS x analytical angles but never the concrete NICHE
639	    # sub-entities a DRB-II rubric names (specific occupations / sectors / demographic groups), so
640	    # their profession-specific primaries are never fetched and the corpus stays canonical-only. This
641	    # AUGMENTS (does not replace) the frontier: ONE bounded LLM call enumerates the named sub-entities
642	    # + deterministic STORM-style disciplinary perspective lenses, each scope-anchored to the question
643	    # so it cannot drift off-subject, and the bounded sub-entity slice is ADDED ON TOP of the FULL
644	    # baseline frontier — the effective query budget is RAISED by the added slice so the sub-entity
645	    # queries never SWAP OUT a baseline query (the Codex/Fable iter-1 REVISE). Every discovered source
646	    # flows through the UNCHANGED per_query_retrieve + frozen faithfulness engine — NEVER auto-trusted.
647	    # Default OFF (PG_SUBENTITY_QUERY_EXPANSION) => byte-identical. §-1.3: adds on-topic queries only,
648	    # drops ZERO sources, touches no faithfulness gate.
649	    #
650	    # Placed AFTER the R5 block (Codex/Fable iter-2 P1 fix). When R2 ran BEFORE R5 it lengthened
651	    # `seed_queries` first, and `language_profile.expand_queries_for_profile` caps its native-language
652	    # additions at max(PG_MULTILINGUAL_MAX_QUERIES, len(base)); once the R2 slice pushed `base` over
653	    # that cap the native-language queries a flag-OFF run WOULD issue were DROPPED — breaking the §-1.3
654	    # strict-superset on non-English (e.g. zh) tasks. Running R5 first means R5 expands the PRE-R2
655	    # baseline — the IDENTICAL input the flag-OFF path expands — into `seed_queries` (English + reserved
656	    # native). `widen_with_sub_entities` then keeps that whole R5-expanded window at the front and only
657	    # RAISES the budget by the sub-entity slice, so the flag-ON issued set is a strict SUPERSET of the
658	    # flag-OFF set: every native-language query is STILL issued (zero dropped) AND the sub-entity queries
659	    # are added. The sub-entity queries are English scope-anchored and route through the unchanged fetch
660	    # un-translated — option-(b): R5 native widening + R2 sub-entity widening both land on the budget;
661	    # translating the sub-entity slice is deliberately NOT done (it would complicate the superset for no
662	    # required gain). Dedup is against the CURRENT (R5-expanded) frontier so no query is issued twice.
663	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import sub_entity_query_expander as _sqe
664	    if _sqe.sub_entity_expansion_enabled() and not _wall_passed():
665	        _sub_qs = _sqe.plan_sub_entity_queries(question, llm)
666	        _new_sub_count = 0
667	        if _sub_qs:
668	            _seed_keys = {(q or "").strip().lower() for q in seed_queries}
669	            _new_sub = [q for q in _sub_qs if (q or "").strip().lower() not in _seed_keys]
670	            _new_sub_count = len(_new_sub)
671	            if _new_sub:
672	                seed_queries, max_queries = _sqe.widen_with_sub_entities(
673	                    list(seed_queries), _new_sub, max_queries
674	                )
675	        # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3a (#1344): sub-entity-expansion ACTIVATION fire marker. Emitted ONLY inside
676	        # this PG_SUBENTITY_QUERY_EXPANSION-gated block => OFF byte-identical. expanded_queries = the NET
677	        # NEW sub-entity queries added on top of the current frontier (0 = LLM named none / all duplicates,
678	        # the eligible-yet-zero signal). Structural presence + count (§-1.3).
679	        logger.info("[activation] subentity_query_expansion: expanded_queries=%d", _new_sub_count)
680	
681	    # COV-C landmark-study expander (I-deepfix-001 Wave-3, #1344): the abstract facet + sub-entity
682	    # frontier never names the LANDMARK empirical studies / RCTs / seminal datasets central to the
683	    # question (the question carries no author names), so the empirical CORE stays absent. ONE bounded
684	    # LLM call enumerates the IN-WINDOW landmark studies (constrained to the question's stated
685	    # publication ceiling `date_end_iso` so a "before June 2023" question seeds the pre-print /
686	    # working-paper version, NEVER a later re-publication) and each scope-anchored query is ADDED ON
687	    # TOP of the FULL baseline frontier — the budget is RAISED by the added slice so a landmark query
688	    # never SWAPS OUT a baseline query (mirrors sub_entity). Every discovered source flows through the
689	    # UNCHANGED per_query_retrieve + frozen faithfulness engine — NEVER auto-trusted. Default OFF
690	    # (PG_LANDMARK_EXPANDER) => byte-identical. §-1.3: adds on-topic in-window queries only, drops ZERO
691	    # sources, touches no faithfulness gate.
692	    # The env flag is read via ``_landmark_expander_enabled()`` (reads PG_LANDMARK_EXPANDER directly,
693	    # NO import) so a flag-OFF run never imports ``landmark_study_expander`` — the OFF path is
694	    # byte-identical even if that module is absent/broken (Codex/Fable Wave-3 P0 defense-in-depth). The
695	    # module import is DEFERRED inside the guard for the same reason.
696	    if _landmark_expander_enabled() and not _wall_passed():
697	        _lm_t0 = time.monotonic()
698	        _new_lm_count = 0
699	        _lm_failed_open = False  # Wave-3b (#1344, Codex P1.1): distinguishes RAN-ok from FAIL-OPEN for the marker
700	        # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 (#1344) — Codex/Fable P1 (additive-on-failure): the ENTIRE landmark lane
701	        # (deferred module import + window extraction + plan + widen) is FAIL-OPEN. If
702	        # ``landmark_study_expander`` is absent or broken, or its bounded LLM planning call raises, the
703	        # lane adds ZERO queries and qgen proceeds on the unchanged baseline frontier — it must NEVER
704	        # abort the whole query generation. This IS the additive / faithfulness-neutral contract: an
705	        # additive coverage lane can only ADD sources, so ANY failure degrades to the flag-OFF
706	        # (zero-added) behaviour, never a hard stop (§-1.3). Also directly covers the Wave-3 P0 concern
707	        # that the FORCE-ON path could ModuleNotFoundError at run time: a missing module now no-ops.
708	        try:
709	            from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import landmark_study_expander as _lse
710	            try:
711	                from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.intake_constraint_extractor import (
712	                    extract_constraints_regex as _extract_constraints_regex,
713	                )
714	                _window_end = _extract_constraints_regex(question).date_end_iso()
715	            except Exception:
716	                _window_end = None
717	            _lm_qs = _lse.plan_landmark_study_queries(question, llm, _window_end)
718	            if _lm_qs:
719	                _seed_keys = {(q or "").strip().lower() for q in seed_queries}
720	                _new_lm = [q for q in _lm_qs if (q or "").strip().lower() not in _seed_keys]
721	                _new_lm_count = len(_new_lm)
722	                if _new_lm:
723	                    seed_queries, max_queries = _lse.widen_with_landmark_studies(
724	                        list(seed_queries), _new_lm, max_queries
725	                    )
726	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — additive lane: any failure adds ZERO, never aborts qgen
727	            # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3b (#1344, Codex P1.1): a FAIL-OPEN landmark lane must NOT read as a
728	            # healthy fire. Set the degrade flag, emit a DISTINCT ``unavailable_failopen`` marker, and
729	            # SUPPRESS the positive ``expanded_queries=N`` marker on this path (below). The run_gate_b
730	            # landmark canary registers this degrade literal as an absent_marker, so a crashed/missing/
731	            # not-recovered expander is REJECTED as dark instead of passing as a healthy added-zero fire.
732	            # The lane STILL fails open (adds zero, never aborts qgen); only the LIVENESS LOG is made
733	            # honest — a lane that FAILED to run is no longer indistinguishable from one that RAN and
734	            # legitimately added zero (§-1.3 anti-dark, faithfulness-neutral).
735	            _lm_failed_open = True
736	            logger.warning(
737	                "[activation] landmark_study_expansion: unavailable_failopen (added 0)", exc_info=True
738	            )
739	            _new_lm_count = 0
740	        # Credit the landmark planning time BACK to the wall so this additive lane rides OUTSIDE the
741	        # baseline budget — the baseline seed frontier issued below (and the R2 completeness loop) get
742	        # the same wall time they would with the lane OFF, so no baseline query is displaced by the
743	        # landmark enumeration's inline LLM call (Codex/Fable P1 "or add budget").
744	        _extra_lane_credit[0] += max(0.0, time.monotonic() - _lm_t0)
745	        # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 (#1344) landmark-expansion ACTIVATION fire marker, Wave-3b (Codex P1.1)
746	        # honesty split. Emitted ONLY inside this PG_LANDMARK_EXPANDER-gated block => OFF byte-identical,
747	        # and ONLY when the lane RAN successfully (``not _lm_failed_open``). expanded_queries = the NET
748	        # NEW in-window landmark queries added on top of the current frontier; 0 here means the expander
749	        # RAN and legitimately added zero (LLM named none / all duplicates / no in-window study) — the
750	        # ACCEPTED eligible-yet-zero signal (§-1.3). On the FAIL-OPEN path this positive marker is
751	        # SUPPRESSED and the ``unavailable_failopen`` degrade marker (above) fires instead, so the canary
752	        # can tell a lane that RAN-and-added-zero (accept) from one that FAILED-and-added-zero (reject as
753	        # dark). Structural presence + count.
754	        if not _lm_failed_open:
755	            logger.info("[activation] landmark_study_expansion: expanded_queries=%d", _new_lm_count)
756	
757	    # GENERAL stance / view-diversification seed lane (default OFF, PG_STANCE_DIVERSIFY_SEEDS): a GENERAL
758	    # deep-research technique — a good report on ANY contested topic must cover multiple viewpoints. For
759	    # each planned facet ALSO issue a small set of queries framing the facet from distinct STANCES
760	    # (supporting / opposing / challenges / opportunities), each scope-anchored to the question. Generic
761	    # topic-agnostic templates only — NO study title / country / benchmark topic is baked in. UNLIKE the
762	    # sub-entity / landmark widen lanes this lane does NOT raise the budget: the net-new stance queries are
763	    # simply APPENDED to the seed frontier and compete for the SAME `max_queries` budget in the seed-issue
764	    # below — if the budget is already full they don't all fit (honest, never a raised cap to fit them).
765	    # De-duplicated against the CURRENT frontier here + against `seen_q` inside the seed-issue loop. §-1.3:
766	    # ADDS on-topic queries only, DROPS ZERO sources, CAPS/THINS/TARGETS NOTHING; every stance query routes
767	    # through the UNCHANGED per_query_retrieve + the FROZEN faithfulness engine — a stance query that finds
768	    # nothing verifiable simply contributes nothing (honest). FAIL-OPEN (Codex/Fable additive-on-failure):
769	    # any import/logic error adds ZERO queries and emits the DISTINCT `unavailable_failopen` degrade marker
770	    # — it NEVER aborts the host query-gen path. The healthy `issued=N` marker reports the REALIZED count of
771	    # net-new stance queries added to the frontier (0 = a legitimate ran-ok-zero — no facet / all duplicates
772	    # — and is ACCEPTED; NEVER gated on a count > 0, §-1.3). Default OFF => this block is skipped entirely =>
773	    # `seed_queries` unchanged => byte-identical.
774	    # Wave-6c (Codex P1): the healthy liveness marker is emitted AFTER _issue_seed_frontier below, keyed on the
775	    # REALIZED count of stance queries that ACTUALLY made it through the (deliberately-unraised) budget + dedup +
776	    # wall — NOT the pre-truncation appended count. Here we only APPEND the net-new stance queries + record their
777	    # keys so the realized count can be measured against the issued set.
778	    _stance_lane_active = False
779	    _stance_keys: set[str] = set()
780	    if _stance_diversify_enabled() and not _wall_passed():
781	        _stance_lane_active = True
782	        try:
783	            _stance_qs = _stance_diversify_seeds(facets, question)
784	            if _stance_qs:
785	                _seed_keys = {(q or "").strip().lower() for q in seed_queries}
786	                _new_stance = [q for q in _stance_qs if (q or "").strip().lower() not in _seed_keys]
787	                if _new_stance:
788	                    _stance_keys = {(q or "").strip().lower() for q in _new_stance}
789	                    # ADD ONLY — append to the frontier; `max_queries` is UNCHANGED (never raised to fit
790	                    # them), so the stance queries flow through the SAME budget-bounded seed-issue below
791	                    # (§-1.3: additive-only, no cap/target — if the budget is full they don't all fit).
792	                    seed_queries = list(seed_queries) + _new_stance
793	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — additive lane: any failure adds ZERO, never aborts qgen
794	            # A FAIL-OPEN stance lane must NOT read as a healthy fire: clear the active flag (SUPPRESSES the
795	            # positive issued= marker below) and emit the DISTINCT `unavailable_failopen` marker so the
796	            # run_gate_b activation canary (which registers the degrade literal as an absent_marker) REJECTS
797	            # the dark lane instead of passing it as a healthy added-zero. The lane STILL fails open (adds
798	            # zero, never aborts qgen); only the liveness LOG is made honest (§-1.3 anti-dark).
799	            _stance_lane_active = False
800	            _stance_keys = set()
801	            logger.warning(
802	                "[activation] stance_diversify_seeds: unavailable_failopen (added 0)", exc_info=True
803	            )
804	
805	    # Issue the seed frontier (facet-angle queries are already full queries — no per-todo llm()
806	    # derivation needed). Record ONLY the queries ACTUALLY issued in the shared seen-set: a
807	    # budget-truncated seed stays eligible for the R2 loop, and the RESERVE angles (deliberately never
808	    # placed in `seed_queries`) stay OUT of `seen_q` so the expansion loop below can fire them for
809	    # still-uncovered facets. Bounded by the compute-safety query budget + the retrieval wall.
810	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 (#1344): the fan-out is bounded-parallel when PG_QGEN_PARALLEL_QUERIES>1
811	    # (order-stable merge, SAME query set); serial + byte-identical at the default 1.
812	    _issued, _issued_results, _issued_rows = _issue_seed_frontier(
813	        seed_queries, seen_q, max_queries - len(queries),
814	        per_query_retrieve, retrieve_kwargs, _wall_passed,
815	    )
816	    queries.extend(_issued)
817	    results.extend(_issued_results)
818	    corpus_rows.extend(_issued_rows)
819	
820	    # Wave-6c (Codex P1): emit the stance-lane liveness marker with the REALIZED issued count — how many
821	    # net-new stance queries ACTUALLY passed the (deliberately-unraised) budget + dedup + wall in
822	    # _issue_seed_frontier, counted from `_issued` (the verbatim issued queries), NOT the pre-truncation
823	    # appended count. Mirrors the qgen_parallel_fanout issued= contract. Suppressed on the fail-open path
824	    # (which logs unavailable_failopen instead). issued=0 is an honest ran-ok-zero — NEVER gated on >0 (§-1.3).
825	    if _stance_lane_active:
826	        _realized_stance = sum(1 for _q in _issued if (_q or "").strip().lower() in _stance_keys)
827	        logger.info("[activation] stance_diversify_seeds: issued=%d", _realized_stance)
828	
829	    # R2: the facet-completeness expansion loop closes gaps on UNCOVERED facets until yield saturates.
830	    if _fc.facet_completeness_enabled() and facets and len(queries) < max_queries and not _wall_passed():
831	        expansion = _fc.run_facet_expansion(
832	            facets,
833	            corpus_rows,
834	            per_query_retrieve,
835	            retrieve_kwargs=retrieve_kwargs,
836	            max_queries=max_queries - len(queries),
837	            already_issued=seen_q,
838	            retrieval_deadline_passed=_wall_passed,
839	        )
840	        queries.extend(expansion.expansion_queries)
841	        results.extend(expansion.results)
842	
843	    return queries, results
844	
845	
846	def plan_fs_researcher_queries(
847	    question: str,
848	    llm: LlmFn,
849	    per_query_retrieve: PerQueryRetrieveFn,
850	    *,
851	    max_queries: int | None = None,
852	    max_rounds: int | None = None,
853	    retrieve_kwargs: dict | None = None,
854	    retrieval_deadline_monotonic: "float | None" = None,
855	) -> tuple[list[str], list[Any]]:
856	    """Run the FS-Researcher TOC/todo-queue + 6-item-checklist loop; return
857	    (queries_issued, per_query_results).
858	
859	    index.md TOC: deconstruct the question into sub-topics (a todo queue). Each round: for every
860	    todo, derive ONE query and retrieve via the production `per_query_retrieve`; then a fixed 6-item
861	    self-review checklist (exhaustive coverage + information density) yields the deficient sub-topics
862	    that become the next round's todos. Stops on NONE or the query budget. Pure control flow over the
863	    injected llm/retrieve — no network or live_retriever import here.
864	
865	    I-deepfix-001 WALL-03 (#1344): ``retrieval_deadline_monotonic`` is the SHARED per-question
866	    retrieval wall (anchored ONCE by the spine, ``None`` when the wall knob is unset). FIX-2 already
867	    short-circuits the per-query ``per_query_retrieve`` once the wall passes; but the adaptive GLM
868	    rounds — the TOC deconstruction, the per-todo query-derivation ``llm()``, and the 6-item checklist
869	    critic ``llm()`` — kept firing past the wall (the Codex iter-1 P1). When the deadline is set and
870	    has passed, the outer round-loop and the inner per-todo loop break, returning the queries gathered
871	    so far. ``None`` (default) => never trips => byte-identical. §-1.3: drops ZERO gathered queries /
872	    sources; the engine is untouched.
873	    """
874	    max_queries = max_queries or _max_queries()
875	    max_rounds = max_rounds or _max_rounds()
876	    retrieve_kwargs = dict(retrieve_kwargs or {})
877	
878	    queries: list[str] = []
879	    results: list[Any] = []
880	    seen_q: set[str] = set()
881	    notes: list[str] = []
882	
883	    # WALL-03: if the wall has ALREADY passed before the first GLM round, skip the loop
884	    # entirely (no TOC-deconstruction llm() either) and hand off the empty query set so the
885	    # spine merges the corpus gathered upstream rather than grinding the GLM TOC call.
886	    if _retrieval_deadline_passed(retrieval_deadline_monotonic):
887	        return queries, results
888	
889	    # R1 (I-deepfix-001, #1344): when the LLM expert-facet planner is flag-enabled, seed the frontier
890	    # from the facet tree (sub-topics x mechanism/stakeholder/counter/temporal/geographic angles,
891	    # each scope-anchored) instead of the single one-query-per-sub-topic TOC. Default OFF => this
892	    # branch never runs and the legacy loop below is byte-identical (the tested path). Isolated in a
893	    # helper so the legacy body is untouched.
894	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import expert_facet_planner as _efp
895	    if _efp.expert_facet_enabled():
896	        return _plan_expert_facet_queries(
897	            question, llm, per_query_retrieve,
898	            max_queries=max_queries, retrieve_kwargs=retrieve_kwargs,
899	            retrieval_deadline_monotonic=retrieval_deadline_monotonic,
900	        )
901	
902	    # index.md TOC: deconstruct the question into sub-topics (the todo queue).
903	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1344): keep every sub-topic scoped to the topic so it does not generalise
904	    # into its broad field (the drb_72 v2 drift). Default-ON; OFF => the legacy bare prompt.
905	    if _scope_anchored():
906	        _toc_prompt = (
907	            "Deconstruct this research topic into sub-topics (the index.md table of contents). "
908	            "Every sub-topic MUST stay within the scope of the topic — carry its subject, domain "
909	            "and key entities; do NOT generalise a sub-topic into its broad field. "
910	            "One sub-topic per line.\n\n" + question
911	        )
912	    else:
913	        _toc_prompt = (
914	            "Deconstruct this research topic into sub-topics (the index.md table of contents). "
915	            "One sub-topic per line.\n\n" + question
916	        )
917	    # I-qgen-001 (#1373): screen the TOC reply for validator/status prose BEFORE it
918	    # becomes the todo queue — a status line must never become a sub-topic.
